objective: Assess understanding of comparing fractions: same denominator, same numerator, equivalent fractions, ordering fractions

questions.question: Compare: 3/8 ○ 5/8
questions.hint: Same denominator — compare numerators.

questions.question: Which fraction is greater: 4/6 or 2/6?
questions.hint: Same denominator — larger numerator = greater fraction.

questions.question: Compare: 1/3 ○ 1/6
questions.hint: Same numerator — smaller denominator = greater fraction.

questions.question: Which fraction is smaller: 1/4 or 1/2?
questions.hint: Same numerator — larger denominator = smaller fraction.

questions.question: Tom ran 5/8 of a mile. Sara ran 3/8 of a mile. Who ran farther?
questions.hint: Same denominator — compare numerators: 5 > 3.

questions.question: Compare: 1/2 ○ 1/8
questions.hint: Same numerator — smaller denominator = bigger piece.

questions.question: Compare: 2/3 ○ 3/4
questions.hint: Use a number line or benchmark 1/2 to compare.

questions.question: Which fraction is greater: 3/4 or 2/3?
questions.hint: 3/4 is close to 1 whole. 2/3 is also close but not as close.

questions.question: Order from least to greatest: 3/4, 1/4, 2/4
questions.hint: Same denominator — order by numerator.

questions.question: Order from greatest to least: 1/2, 1/6, 1/3
questions.hint: Same numerator — smaller denominator = greater fraction.

questions.question: Which fraction is equivalent to 1/2?
questions.hint: Equivalent fractions name the same amount. 1/2 = ?/4

questions.question: Which pair shows equivalent fractions?
questions.hint: Equivalent fractions cover the same area of a whole.

questions.question: Which fraction is equivalent to 2/4?
questions.hint: 2/4 and 1/2 name the same point on a number line.

questions.question: Compare: 2/4 ○ 1/2
questions.hint: 2/4 and 1/2 are equivalent fractions.

questions.question: Maya ate 3/6 of a granola bar. Leo ate 4/6 of the same size bar. Who ate more?
questions.hint: Same denominator — compare numerators: 4 > 3.

questions.question: Aiden drank 1/3 of a bottle of water. Emma drank 1/4 of the same size bottle. Who drank more?
questions.hint: Same numerator — 1/3 has a smaller denominator so each piece is bigger.

questions.question: Order from least to greatest: 1/2, 1/8, 1/4, 1/3
questions.hint: Same numerator — order by denominator from greatest to least denominator = least to greatest fraction.

questions.question: Which TWO fractions are equivalent to 1/2?
questions.hint: 1/2 = 2/4 = 3/6. Each names the same amount.

questions.question: Jake says 3/4 > 5/6 because 4 < 6. Is Jake correct? Why?
choices: Yes — smaller denominator always means greater fraction
No — you cannot compare fractions with different numerators AND denominators this way
Yes — 3/4 is always greater than any fraction with 6 in the denominator
questions.hint: Jake's rule only works when numerators are the same. Use a number line to compare 3/4 and 5/6.

questions.question: Three friends ran different distances: Ana ran 3/4 mile, Ben ran 2/3 mile, and Cara ran 5/6 mile. Who ran the farthest?
questions.hint: Compare each to the benchmark 1 whole: 5/6 is closest to 1.
